t_ch: 列车，真的在天上飞呢…下次克拉拉一定要说服史瓦罗先生一起来。
t_en: The Express really does fly up in the sky... Next time, I'll have to convince Mr. Svarog to come along.

t_ch: 看来克拉拉很喜欢列车。
t_en: Seems you like the Express very much.

t_ch: 喜欢！
t_en: I love it!

t_ch: 谢谢你邀请克拉拉过来，{NICKNAME}{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！
t_en: Thank you for bringing me along, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 我刚刚从姬子大姐姐那听说，原来列车曾经搁浅过……
t_en: Himeko just told me that the Express has been stranded before...

t_ch: 为了让列车重新动起来，花了不少功夫，很辛苦。
t_en: She said it took a lot of hard work to get it back on track. I bet it was difficult.

t_ch: 平时的维护也是…如果可以的话，也让克拉拉来帮忙维护列车吧！
t_en: Not to mention routine maintenance... If possible, you should let me help fix the Express, too!

t_ch: 怎么能麻烦客人呢…
t_en: How could we trouble our guests...?

t_ch: 不麻烦！克拉拉还有很多知识要学习，有很多问题想向姬子大姐姐和帕姆请教。
t_en: No, it's no trouble at all! I've still got lots to learn, and lots of questions to ask Miss Himeko and Pom-Pom.

t_ch: 比如…怎么才可以让帕金斯它们飞起来，像列车一样飞到更高的地方呢？
t_en: Like... How can we get Perkins to fly — how can we get him to fly to super high places like the Express?

t_ch: 想想就很期待~
t_en: Looking forward to it~

t_ch: 听姬子大姐姐说……
t_en: I heard Miss Himeko say...

t_ch: 克拉拉还有很多知识要学习，有很多问题想向姬子大姐姐和帕姆请教。
t_en: I've still got lots to learn, and lots of questions to ask Miss Himeko and Pom-Pom.

t_ch: 比如…怎么才可以让帕金斯它们飞起来，像列车一样飞到更高的地方呢？
t_en: Like... How can we get Perkins to fly — how can we get him to fly to super high places like the Express?

t_ch: 想想就很期待~
t_en: Looking forward to it~

t_ch: 之前，克拉拉问了史瓦罗先生要不要来列车上参观，结果还是被史瓦罗先生回绝了……
t_en: I asked Mr. Svarog if he'd like to come with me. But he said he couldn't...

t_ch: 「我还要继续监测影响贝洛伯格存续的相关风险因子，不能离开雅利洛-Ⅵ。」史瓦罗先生说：「但克拉拉可以将自己所见转述给我。我会将结果录入数据库。」
t_en: He said: "I must continue to monitor the risk factors endangering Belobog. I cannot leave Jarilo-VI." But he also said that I could recount my experience on the Express and he'd store the information in his database.

t_ch: 所以，克拉拉正在想办法把这里看到的风景记录下来…但是，可以说的东西实在是太多了，克拉拉的眼睛都要看花了……
t_en: That's why I want to find a way to record the scenery, it's just... there's so much to see. My eyes can't take it all in...

t_ch: 用手机拍个照吧。
t_en: Why not take some pictures with your phone?

t_ch: …啊！克拉拉完全没想到…谢谢{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}！
t_en: Ah! How come I never thought of that? Thanks {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 慢慢来，别着急。
t_en: It can be a little overwhelming. Take your time.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，谢谢{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}……
t_en: Haha, thanks, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: …啊！克拉拉突然想到办法了——克拉拉可以用手机把这里的风景拍成照片，再拿给史瓦罗先生看！
t_en: ...Oh! I've got an idea. I can take some pictures with my phone and show them to Mr. Svarog!

t_ch: 手机、手机…克拉拉之前把手机放到哪里去了呢……
t_en: Speaking of my phone, where did I put it...

t_ch: 克拉拉要把这风景记下来……
t_en: I'll record this scenery then...

t_ch: 克拉拉正在想办法把这里看到的风景记录下来！但、但是……
t_en: I'm trying to capture the views but...

t_ch: 克拉拉…好像忘记把手机带来了……
t_en: I think I forgot to bring my phone with me...

t_ch: 帕姆…走起来一扭一扭的，好可爱喔……
t_en: Pom-Pom looks really cute when they walk...

t_ch: 而且…帕姆看起来毛绒绒的，摸起来手感也很好……
t_en: Not to mention how fluffy and soft they are...

t_ch: …好想…好想把帕姆抱回去！
t_en: I wish I could take Pom-Pom back to Belobog!

t_ch: 帕姆下不了车哦。
t_en: It's a shame Pom-Pom can't leave the train.

t_ch: 唔…帕姆也对克拉拉这么说了，好可惜……
t_en: Yeah... they did mention that to me. I wish they could...

t_ch: 其实帕姆很恐怖的…
t_en: Pom-Pom can be scary sometimes...

t_ch: 唔…克拉拉想要进引擎室看看的时候，帕姆生气的样子确实很恐怖……
t_en: You're right... I poked my head into the engine room and Pom-Pom acted like an angry puppy...

t_ch: …但是…但是帕姆还是很可爱的！
t_en: ...Anyway, it's nice to spend time with such a cute conductor!

t_ch: 帕姆好可爱……
t_en: Pom-Pom is so cute...

t_ch: 帕姆…走起来一扭一扭的，好可爱喔……
t_en: Pom-Pom looks really cute when they walk...

t_ch: 而且…帕姆看起来毛绒绒的，摸起来手感也很好……
t_en: Not to mention how fluffy and soft they are...

t_ch: …好想…好想把帕姆抱回去！
t_en: I wish I could take Pom-Pom back to Belobog!

t_ch: 刚刚，瓦尔特先生给克拉拉讲了好多好多、其他星球的机器人电影故事！
t_en: I just finished talking to Mr. Yang. He was telling me about movies on other planets that have robots in them!

t_ch: 电影里的机器人都好厉害哦！大家都好高、好大…还有翅膀！
t_en: They sounded so cool! They're really huge and they have wings!

t_ch: 还可以飞上天…飞到宇宙里哦！真的好厉害！
t_en: They launch themselves into the sky and even into outer space!

t_ch: 克拉拉在想…要是给史瓦罗先生安装一副翅膀的话，是不是也可以像故事里一样，飞到宇宙里了呢？
t_en: I'm thinking... If I put a pair of wings on Mr. Svarog, would he be able to fly around the universe like in the movies?

t_ch: 感觉可以试试。
t_en: Why not give it a go?

t_ch: 唔…如果{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}也这么说，克拉拉心里就有底了。
t_en: Mm... If you think so too, then I'll definitely try. Thanks, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 感觉有点难…
t_en: Doesn't sound like an easy task...

t_ch: 唔…虽然{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}也这么说，但克拉拉还是想试试看……
t_en: Mm... are you sure, {NICKNAME}? I still want to give it a try...

t_ch: 史瓦罗，出击！
t_en: Svarog to the rescue!

t_ch: 喔！感觉、感觉是电影里会出现的那种，很酷的台词！
t_en: Yeah! Sounds like one of those cool movie lines!

t_ch: 如果克拉拉成功了…飞到宇宙里的时候，也要说这样的台词！
t_en: If I can fly around the universe one day too, I'll come up with my own cool thing to say!

t_ch: 总之…克拉拉会去找史瓦罗先生问问看的！
t_en: Anyway... I'll talk to Mr. Svarog first and see if he likes the idea!

t_ch: 如果史瓦罗先生会飞……
t_en: If Mr. Svarog could fly...

t_ch: 电影里的机器人都好厉害哦！大家都好高、好大…还有翅膀！
t_en: The movie robots sound so cool! They're really huge and they have wings!

t_ch: 还可以飞上天…飞到宇宙里哦！真的好厉害！
t_en: They launch themselves into the sky and even into outer space!

t_ch: 克拉拉在想…要是给史瓦罗先生安装一副翅膀的话，是不是也可以像故事里一样，飞到宇宙里了呢？
t_en: I'm thinking... If I put a pair of wings on Mr. Svarog, would he be able to fly around the universe like in the movies?

t_ch: 啊，是{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！克拉拉有事想要找你……
t_en: Oh, it's you! I wanted to show you something...

t_ch: 什么事？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 是不是要送我星琼了？
t_en: A gift of Stellar Jade?

t_ch: 唔…星琼是什么？克拉拉好像没听过这个名字……
t_en: Huh? Stellar Jade? What's that?

t_ch: 其实…克拉拉是想说——不知道{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}还记得帕斯卡吗？
t_en: Actually... I wanted to ask if you remember Pascal.

t_ch: 前阵子，克拉拉又去了一趟铆钉镇的「淬火工坊」…在那里发现了几块很漂亮的小石头，还有一张纸条。
t_en: I went to the Tempering Workshop in Rivet Town a while ago and found some nice-looking pebbles — there was a note on the side.

t_ch: 纸条上是帕斯卡的字迹…它似乎想把这些石头送给{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}呢。
t_en: It's Pascal's handwriting — he says he wanted to send the pebbles to you as a gift.

t_ch: 虽然不知道这是什么…但还请{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}收下！毕竟这是他的心意……
t_en: I'm not sure what the pebbles are used for... but please take them! It would make Pascal happy.

t_ch: …对了，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}有空的话，也可以回机械聚落看看哦。
t_en: ...Oh! When you have time, don't forget to come back to Robot Settlement and pay us a visit.

t_ch: 希望你能喜欢……
t_en: Hope you like it...

t_ch: 希望{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}会喜欢这些漂亮的小石头……
t_en: I hope you like these pebbles, {F#big sister}{M#big brother} {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: …对了，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}有空的话，也可以回机械聚落看看哦。
t_en: ...Oh! When you have time, don't forget to come back to Robot Settlement and pay us a visit.

t_ch: 哎呀，车厢也太酷了吧，那儿是不是可以摆个小舞台了？
t_en: Oh wow, the train carriages are so cool. Can we put a little stage there?

t_ch: 没问题，这事交给我。
t_en: No problem. Leave it to me.

t_ch: 我问问其他人的意见。
t_en: Um, I'll have to ask around first.

t_ch: 哈哈，我开玩笑的。
t_en: Haha, I'm only kidding.

t_ch: 不过要是你们愿意，我随时可以给你们来一场小型私人演出。
t_en: But if you like, I could give you all a small private show any time.

t_ch: 这儿的沙发都是现成的，当作贵宾席正合适。
t_en: The sofas here all look tailor-made — perfect for VIP guests!

t_ch: 期待宇宙中的摇滚演出！
t_en: Looking forward to rocking out in space!

t_ch: 还是要谢谢{NICKNAME}邀请我过来——虽然我现在暂时脱不开身…但我也很期待列车之旅哦！
t_en: Thanks for inviting me, {NICKNAME}. I've got my hands full right now, but I'm looking forward to the Express tour!

t_ch: 车厢也太酷了吧！
t_en: The carriage is so cool!

t_ch: 要是你们愿意，我随时可以给你们来一场小型私人演出。
t_en: If you like, I can give all of you a small private show any time.

t_ch: 这儿的沙发都是现成的，当作贵宾席正合适。
t_en: The sofas here all look tailor-made — perfect for VIP guests!

t_ch: 期待宇宙中的摇滚演出！
t_en: Looking forward to rocking out in space!

t_ch: 还是要谢谢{NICKNAME}邀请我过来——虽然我现在暂时脱不开身…但我也很期待列车之旅哦！
t_en: Thanks for inviting me, {NICKNAME}. I've got my hands full right now, but I'm looking forward to the Express tour!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，能不能和你打个商量，列车留声机借我带回家几天？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, can I borrow the Express' phonograph for a few days?

t_ch: 你不会想把它拆了吧？
t_en: You're not going to take it to pieces, are you?

t_ch: 你当我是什么人啊……
t_en: Who do you take me for...

t_ch: 好吧，我承认有那么几秒钟，我产生过这样的想法。
t_en: Alright, I admit that for a few seconds there, the thought may have crossed my mind.

t_ch: 我懂，歌太好听了吧？
t_en: I understand. The music here is pretty good, huh?

t_ch: 而且不仅有贝洛伯格的音乐，还有其他星球的……
t_en: And not only do you have Belobog music, but music from other planets, too...

t_ch: 你们从哪弄来的好宝贝？
t_en: I'd love to know where you picked this thing up.

t_ch: 哎，我也知道这事儿肯定不可能……
t_en: *sigh*, I'm not sure you guys will agree, but...

t_ch: 不过，如果我为了听音乐专程来一次星穹列车，你们会介意么？
t_en: ...would you mind if I made a trip to the Astral Express one time, just to listen to music?

t_ch: 没关系，我可以陪你。
t_en: No problem, it'd be nice to hang out.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}就是{NICKNAME}，答应得真爽快！
t_en: I should've known you'd be cool with it, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 说好了，下次来机械屋找我，姐姐给你免费！
t_en: It's a deal then. Next time you need something done in the workshop, I'll do it for free!

t_ch: 再看一眼留声机……
t_en: Another look at the phonograph...

t_ch: 如果我为了听音乐专程来一次星穹列车，你们会介意么？
t_en: Would you mind if I made a trip to the Astral Express, just to listen to music?

t_ch: 没关系，我可以陪你。
t_en: No problem, it'd be nice to hang out.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}就是{NICKNAME}，答应得真爽快！
t_en: I should've known you'd be cool with it, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 说好了，下次来机械屋找我，姐姐给你免费！
t_en: It's a deal then. Next time you need something done in the workshop, I'll do it for free!

t_ch: 哟，{NICKNAME}！来得挺巧，我刚和姬子小姐聊完天呢。
t_en: Right on time, {NICKNAME}! I just finished chatting with Himeko.

t_ch: 都聊什么了？
t_en: What were you two talking about?

t_ch: 你俩肯定挺投缘的吧。
t_en: I knew you two would hit it off.

t_ch: 哈哈，那当然！就算只为了姬子小姐和她的…藏品，我都得时不时来列车坐坐呢。
t_en: Haha, of course! Himeko and her... collection — more than enough reason to come visit the Express.

t_ch: 我们聊了好多——从她怎么修复这辆列车，到她跟着列车经历的冒险，再到那位毛绒绒的列车长帕姆和你们……
t_en: We talked about a lot of things — how she repaired the Express, all about her adventures, a certain resident fluffy conductor, and you guys...

t_ch: 哦，对了！她还专门给我看了看她的杰作…那可真是太——酷啦！看得我实在手痒痒，可惜我也不好意思和人说能不能拆开看看……
t_en: Oh, and she showed me her masterpiece too. I gotta say: Soooo cool! It was hard to resist the urge to open it up and see what was inside, but I managed to control myself...

t_ch: 唔，要是跟着你们就能见到这么多新奇玩意…那我要不还是一起走算了？
t_en: If I'd known there were so many high-tech wonders on the Express, I probably would've insisted on going with you guys.

t_ch: 哈哈，开个玩笑，贝洛伯格还需要我呢。等哪天我的才华耗尽了，再走也不迟——你们可要等着我啊！说好了哦！
t_en: Haha, only joking — I'm needed in Belobog. Still, if I ever forget how to play the guitar or fix things in my workshop... there'll be a spot for me here, right? It's a deal!

t_ch: 和姬子小姐聊了聊……
t_en: Chatted with Himeko...

t_ch: 姬子小姐专门给我看了看她的杰作…那可真是太——酷啦！看得我实在手痒痒，可惜我也不好意思和人说能不能拆开看看……
t_en: Himeko showed me her masterpiece. I gotta say: Soooo cool! It was hard to resist the urge to open it up and see what was inside, but I managed to control myself...

t_ch: 唔，要是跟着你们就能见到这么多新奇玩意…那我要不还是一起走算了？
t_en: If I'd known there were so many high-tech wonders on the Express, I probably would've insisted on going with you guys.

t_ch: 哎，{NICKNAME}，平时瓦尔特先生是个怎么样的人？
t_en: Hey {NICKNAME}, how would you describe Mr. Yang?

t_ch: 老成持重的前辈。
t_en: A wise and cautious mentor.

t_ch: 咦？那看来你还不知道他的另一面……
t_en: Oh? Seems you haven't seen his other side...

t_ch: 渴望冒险的热血男儿。
t_en: A child at heart who loves adventures.

t_ch: 嗯…这倒是还挺贴切。真想不到这样一个男人，居然会对那种东西感兴趣……
t_en: He doesn't look like the type, but that sounds about right.

t_ch: 大机器人狂热爱好者。
t_en: Fanatical fan of big robots.

t_ch: 对对对，就是这个！我跟你说啊……
t_en: Exactly! You won't believe this, but...

t_ch: 刚才我和瓦尔特先生聊天，聊到我是个机械师，以前还给银鬃铁卫供过职；后来不知怎地，我们就聊到那个「造物引擎」……
t_en: I was talking to Mr. Yang about my career as a mechanic and my time in the Silvermane Guards. Somehow, we got onto that so-called Engine of Creation...

t_ch: 结果瓦尔特先生噌地一下就来精神了，把我吓了一跳！他拼命向我打听「造物引擎」技术规格参数如何，有没有乘坐过之类的…可惜我也没见过那台大机器人。
t_en: As soon as I started talking about it, he jumped out of his seat in excitement — scared me half to death. He wanted to know everything there was to know about its technical specs and whether I'd ever had the chance to ride in it... Sadly, I had to tell him I never saw the robot myself.

t_ch: 哎，说得我也好奇起来了…要不，干脆找个时间去北方山谷里看看好了！
t_en: You know what? He's got me excited too... Let's find a time to head into the northern valleys and check it out!

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生找我打听了半天……
t_en: Mr. Yang asked me about it all day...

t_ch: 「造物引擎」…可惜我也没见过那台大机器人。
t_en: The Engine of Creation... Sadly, I never got the chance to see the robot myself.

t_ch: 哎，说得我也好奇起来了…要不，干脆找个时间去北方山谷里看看好了！
t_en: You know what? Mr. Yang got me excited about it too... Let's find a time to head into the northern valleys and check it out!

t_ch: 啧…实在想不到啊……
t_en: Tsk... Think, Serval, think...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你说——如果要给你们星穹列车写一首歌，该起什么名字好呢？
t_en: Hey {NICKNAME}, if you were to write a song about the Express, what would you name it?

t_ch: 《夜间列车》？
t_en: The Night Train?

t_ch: 嗯…感觉不错啊。听起来像是什么老牌乐队的巡演开场曲……
t_en: Hmm... not bad. Sounds like something one of those old bands would play at the beginning of their sets...

t_ch: 《疯狂列车》？
t_en: The Hype Train?

t_ch: 嗯…感觉不错啊。虽然听起来有点俗，但让我想起了一位叫兰迪的传奇吉他手——可惜他已经去世了……
t_en: Haha, love it, but I bet there's a lot of songs with that name. It sounds like something Randy would play — he's one of my favorite legendary guitarists. May he rest in peace...

t_ch: 《高速之星》！
t_en: Highway Star!

t_ch: 呃…听起来有点生猛。你们这列车也能在高速公路上飚起来吗？
t_en: Um... not quite my taste. "High speed" doesn't quite do the Express justice.

t_ch: 谢谢你的建议！有了这个点子，得让我好好斟酌一下……
t_en: Anyway, thanks for the suggestions! They all have something about them. I'll have to think about it...

t_ch: 起个什么名字好呢……
t_en: What's a good name, then...

t_ch: 得让我好好斟酌一下……
t_en: Still can't decide which name to go for...

t_ch: 有了这么多好点子，感觉真是越来越难挑了，让我好好斟酌一下……
t_en: It's hard to say which name I like the best — they all have something about them. I'll have to think about it...

t_ch: 哎…最近手头清闲了些，就想着也该写新歌了。结果我思来想去，始终没个能用的灵感……
t_en: *sigh* I've had a lot of free time recently, so I was thinking about writing some new songs. Sadly, that's about as far as I got...

t_ch: 所以——我就来找你们玩啦！怎么样，这么久没见，有没有想姐姐啊～
t_en: But at least I have time to come visit you guys! Did you miss me?

t_ch: 太想了。
t_en: You have no idea.

t_ch: 哈，我就知道你会这么说！来来来，我给你带了点礼物——
t_en: Ha! I knew you'd say that. Here, I've got a gift for you...

t_ch: …咦？我放哪儿了来着？
t_en: ...aaaand where did I leave it?

t_ch: 也没有很想。
t_en: Meh.

t_ch: 真的？成吧，那我这礼物算是白带了……
t_en: For real? Hmph, never mind the GIFT I got for you then...

t_ch: 这就是你偷懒的借口？
t_en: You sure you're not just ignoring your writer's block?

t_ch: 瞧你这话说的！什么偷懒啊——这叫换换脑子，找找灵感！
t_en: You can't work your way out of writer's block! A short break is a good way of refreshing the mind.

t_ch: 没想到我在你眼里就是这种形象，早知道就不给你带礼物了……
t_en: Hmph! Didn't think you'd accuse me of goofing off... Never mind the GIFT I got for you then...

t_ch: 嘿，开个玩笑——来，给你！
t_en: Ha, just kidding — here you goooo~

t_ch: 这可是贝洛伯格著名摇滚明星为你特意准备的礼物，可别嫌弃啊——就算嫌弃也别让我知道，好吧？
t_en: A gift from Belobog's number one rock star — you're not allowed to dislike it! Or at least don't tell me if that's the case...

t_ch: 最近没什么灵感啊……
t_en: Argh, this writer's block...

t_ch: 在这里转了这么久，好像还是没个能用的灵感…到底是怎么回事？
t_en: Hmm, I've been on the Express for a while but I still don't have any new songwriting inspiration. How strange...

t_ch: 难道是因为…我已经抵达事业巅峰了吗？
t_en: Could it be that... I've peaked already?

t_ch: 还是说，要再送{NICKNAME}一次礼物？！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, how about you give me some ideas... and I get you some more gifts?

t_ch: 抱歉，最近事务繁忙，列车之旅耽搁许久，总算成行了。
t_en: Apologies, I've been busy lately so I had to put off the Express tour for a while. Here we are, finally.

t_ch: 都是朋友，不必道歉。
t_en: We're all friends here — no need to apologize.

t_ch: 抱歉…呃…对不起，我是说，你明白我的意思……
t_en: Sorry — uh... forgive me — I mean... you know what I mean...

t_ch: 都是朋友，来了就好。
t_en: We're friends, I'm just glad you could make it.

t_ch: 正因为是朋友，心底多少有些过意不去……
t_en: It's because we're friends that I feel a little guilty...

t_ch: 接下来要怎么参观？按照列车游览路线么？
t_en: What should we see next? Do we just follow the Express tour route?

t_ch: 跟我来就好。
t_en: Come with me.

t_ch: 好，那就听{NICKNAME}你的了。
t_en: Alright, lead the way, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 能受邀来列车，我感到十分荣幸。
t_en: I'm honored to be invited aboard the Express.

t_ch: ——这是真话，不是和你在客气……
t_en: Truly, I'm not just being polite with you...

t_ch: 总算成行了……
t_en: Time to depart...

t_ch: 列车之旅总算成行了…接下来要怎么参观？按照列车游览路线么？
t_en: It's finally time for the Express tour... What should we see next? Do we just follow the Express tour route?

t_ch: 跟我来就好。
t_en: Come with me.

t_ch: 好，那就听{NICKNAME}你的了。
t_en: Alright, lead the way, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 能受邀来列车，我感到十分荣幸。
t_en: I'm honored to be invited aboard the Express.

t_ch: ——这是真话，不是和你在客气……
t_en: Truly, I'm not just being polite with you...

t_ch: 朗道家是个大家族，家里经常有人来来往往。
t_en: The Landau family is big — there are always people around at home.

t_ch: 列车和我家的感觉不太一样，挺空旷的。
t_en: The Express doesn't give me that same feeling. It's pretty empty.

t_ch: 好像不是什么坏事？
t_en: That's not a bad thing, right?

t_ch: 对。虽然空旷，但到处都有鲜活的生活痕迹。我能想到当大家都在的时候，它是如何活起来的。
t_en: Not at all. It may be empty, but there are traces of life everywhere. I can just imagine how bustling it would be with people here.

t_ch: 打个比方，就像深夜的贝洛伯格，空旷，宁静，却始终亲切。
t_en: It's like Belobog in the dead of the night: Empty, quiet, but always kind.

t_ch: 这种感觉对于一个从没离开过贝洛伯格的人来说，真的很神奇。
t_en: It's an amazing feeling for someone who's never left Belobog before.

t_ch: 列车的感觉不太一样……
t_en: The Express feels quite different...

t_ch: 列车虽然空旷，但到处都有鲜活的生活痕迹。我能想到当大家都在的时候，它是如何活起来的。
t_en: The Express is pretty empty, but there are traces of life everywhere. I can just imagine how bustling it would be with people here.

t_ch: 打个比方，就像深夜的贝洛伯格，空旷，宁静，却始终亲切。
t_en: It's like Belobog in the dead of the night: Empty, quiet, but always kind.

t_ch: 这种感觉对于一个从没离开过贝洛伯格的人来说，真的很神奇。
t_en: It's an amazing feeling for someone who's never left Belobog before.

t_ch: 参观列车过后，我就在想……
t_en: Ever since the Express tour, I've been wondering...

t_ch: …如果铁卫能拥有一台这样的列车，会不会对战事有所帮助。
t_en: If the Guards had a train like this, would it make our campaigns easier?

t_ch: 可以用来运输物资。
t_en: You could use it to transfer supplies.

t_ch: 运输物资…吗？听起来确实很方便……
t_en: Transfer supplies... huh. That's not a bad idea.

t_ch: 可以用来调度士兵。
t_en: You could use it to deploy troops.

t_ch: 调度士兵…吗？听起来确实很快捷……
t_en: Deploy troops... huh. That's not a bad idea.

t_ch: 把怪物们通通撞飞！！！
t_en: You could drive it straight into the monsters and send them flying!!!

t_ch: …你怎么突然这么激动？
t_en: ...I don't think I've ever seen you that excited.

t_ch: 而且…你竟然要把列车送进战场吗？我只能想到和后勤调度相关的用法……
t_en: So... you're thinking of turning the Express into a combat unit? Personally I'd only use it for logistics...

t_ch: 嗯…被你这么一说，我感觉也许值得和技术部的铁卫们聊聊列车的事。
t_en: Hmm... You've given me some food for thought — I should have a chat with the Technology Division about this.

t_ch: 这次回去就把这件事提上日程吧。
t_en: I'll add it to my to-do list.

t_ch: 如果铁卫有这样的列车……
t_en: If the Guards had a train like this...

t_ch: 我感觉…也许值得和技术部的铁卫们聊聊列车的事。
t_en: It might be worth talking to the Technology Division about incorporating a train into our units.

t_ch: …这次回去就把这件事提上日程吧。
t_en: ...I should add it to my to-do list.

t_ch: 刚才与姬子小姐及瓦尔特先生聊了会儿天，学到了许多有趣的事物。
t_en: I just had quite an interesting conversation with Himeko and Mr. Yang.

t_ch: 只是…姬子小姐手制的那个…黑色的饮料，味道实在……
t_en: But that brown drink that Himeko makes... it's very...

t_ch: 我非常理解你…
t_en: It's not for everyone...

t_ch: …看来是那饮料的风味确实不对。以后我还是别碰它了……
t_en: ...I'm not alone then. I'll be keeping a safe distance.

t_ch: 但无论如何，和两位前辈聊天使我受益良多，对此我表示非常感激。
t_en: Anyway, I learned a lot from them. I'm very grateful for that.

t_ch: 都聊了些什么？
t_en: What did you guys talk about?

t_ch: 嗯…最令我震惊的果然还是「星神」了。
t_en: Well, the part that shocked me the most was about the Aeons.

t_ch: 爱喝就多喝点。
t_en: There's plenty more where that came from.

t_ch: 我…咳。说是讨厌好像有点不礼貌…但我也确实不喜欢那个味道。
t_en: Ahem... I'm okay, thanks. Not what I'm used to.

t_ch: 但无论如何，和两位前辈聊天使我受益良多，对此我表示非常感激。
t_en: Anyway, I learned a lot from them. I'm very grateful for that.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格的筑城者们总会聊起克里珀的丰功伟绩，在我们心中，祂就是全宇宙唯一至高无上的存在。
t_en: Belobog's Architects talk about the great deeds of Qlipoth all the time. In our minds, Qlipoth represents the single greatest power in the universe.

t_ch: 但姬子小姐与瓦尔特先生告诉我，原来宇宙中远不止克里珀一位「星神」——就连那在七百年前入侵贝洛伯格的异星敌寇，也是某位「星神」的令使。
t_en: But Himeko and Mr. Yang told me that Qlipoth is far from the only Aeon in the universe. For example, the enemies that invaded Belobog <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago were Emanators of a different Aeon.

t_ch: 现在，贝洛伯格的自然环境现正在逐步恢复正常，那些令使随时都有可能卷土重来……
t_en: Things are getting back on track for Belobog, but those Emanators could return one day...

t_ch: …看来，是时候让银鬃铁卫做好准备了。
t_en: ...I need to make sure the Silvermane Guards are always at the ready.

t_ch: 还有克里珀之外的星神……
t_en: And the other Aeons apart from Qlipoth...

t_ch: 姬子小姐与瓦尔特先生告诉我，原来宇宙中远不止克里珀一位「星神」——就连那在七百年前入侵贝洛伯格的异星敌寇，也是某位「星神」的令使。
t_en: Himeko and Mr. Yang told me that Qlipoth is far from the only Aeon in the universe. For example, the enemies that invaded Belobog <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago were Emanators of another Aeon.

t_ch: 现在，贝洛伯格的自然环境现正在逐步恢复正常，那些令使随时都有可能卷土重来……
t_en: Things are getting back on track for Belobog, but those Emanators could return one day...

t_ch: …看来，是时候让银鬃铁卫做好准备了。
t_en: ...I need to make sure the Silvermane Guards are always at the ready.

t_ch: 不久前，姬子小姐给我展示了她的收藏。其中有一个叫「卫星」的东西，我很感兴趣。
t_en: Not long ago, Himeko showed me her collection. There was something called a "satellite" — I was very intrigued.

t_ch: 我觉得，它和我们铁卫使用的自动机兵有些类似。如果我们能把自动机兵像「卫星」一样部署到太空中，就能在战斗中取得优势。
t_en: It works in a similar way to our automatons. If we could launch our automatons into space like satellites, it'd definitely give us an edge in combat.

t_ch: 可以预警潜在的威胁。
t_en: You'd be able to detect potential threats.

t_ch: 你说得对。自动机兵可以成为我们在太空中的眼睛——不管威胁来自天外还是地面，它们都能第一时间发现。
t_en: That's right — the automatons would be our eyes and ears in space. No matter whether the threats arose on the surface or in outer space, we'd be able to spot them.

t_ch: 可以出其不意地发动攻击。
t_en: You'd be able to act preemptively.

t_ch: 你说得对。自动机兵可以成为我们在太空中的炮台——就像姬子小姐的藏品那样。
t_en: Exactly — the automatons would be our turrets in space. That's also how Himeko uses her satellite.

t_ch: 太难实现了吧……
t_en: Sounds like it'll require a whole lot of engineering...

t_ch: 如果只凭我们贝洛伯格现有的技术，确实很难实现。不过……
t_en: It will be very difficult to bring this concept to reality using Belobog's current technology.

t_ch: 姬子小姐和我说，如果银鬃铁卫有意如此，可以随时向她请求帮助。
t_en: But Himeko told me that if the Silvermane Guards do go ahead with it, she'll be glad to help.

t_ch: 只是此事我一人说了不算。我还得将提案整理好，与布洛妮娅、佩拉和其他尉官商议才是……
t_en: Only thing though is, I can't make the call. I'll need to write a report and discuss it with Bronya, Pela, and others first.

t_ch: 如果把自动机兵部署在太空……
t_en: If we deploy automatons into space...

t_ch: 如果我们能把自动机兵像「卫星」一样部署到太空中，就能在战斗中取得优势。
t_en: If we could launch our automatons into space like satellites, it'd definitely give us an edge in combat.

t_ch: 只是此事我一人说了不算。我还得将提案整理好，与布洛妮娅、佩拉和其他尉官商议才是……
t_en: Only thing though is, I can't make the call. I'll need to write a report and discuss it with Bronya, Pela, and others first.

t_ch: 在这列车前后车厢转了好久，终于找到你了。
t_en: Oh, you are here. I've been looking for you up and down the train.

t_ch: 找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Anything I can do for you?

t_ch: 其实…是我带了些礼物想要给你。
t_en: Actually... I brought you a few things.

t_ch: 没闯进资料室吧？
t_en: You didn't break into the archives, did you?

t_ch: 呃…其实差一点点……
t_en: Um... nearly.

t_ch: 我带了些礼物给你们，就当赔礼吧。
t_en: I brought you all some gifts. Hope I didn't cause too much trouble.

t_ch: 其实我也在找你。
t_en: I've been looking for you, too.

t_ch: 那还挺巧的…我带了些礼物给你们。
t_en: Is that so? Well, I've got something for you.

t_ch: 这是铁卫全军为报答列车拯救了贝洛伯格，特意为你们准备的礼物。
t_en: This is a gift from the Silvermane Guards, to show our gratitude towards those who saved Belobog.

t_ch: 有机会的话，常回贝洛伯格看看吧——银鬃铁卫随时欢迎你们归来。
t_en: When you find a moment, do come back to Belobog to have a chat. The Silvermane Guards will always welcome you.

t_ch: 这是铁卫全体的心意……
t_en: The Silvermane Guards wish to express their gratitude...

t_ch: 这是铁卫全军为报答列车拯救了贝洛伯格，特意为你们准备的礼物。
t_en: This is a gift from the Silvermane Guards, to show our gratitude towards those who saved Belobog.

t_ch: 有机会的话，常回贝洛伯格看看吧——银鬃铁卫随时欢迎你们归来。
t_en: When you find a moment, do come back to Belobog to have a chat. The Silvermane Guards will always welcome you.

t_ch: 这里可以增加一些虹膜识别设备，防止外来人员潜入列车……
t_en: This place could do with some iris scanners... wouldn't want unregistered visitors sneaking aboard.

t_ch: 我记得艾丝妲小姐之前有定制过一些设备，或许可以问问她……
t_en: I remember Lady Asta commissioned some security equipment a while back. Maybe she still has some spares...

t_ch: 外…外来人员！？
t_en: U—Unregistered visitors!?

t_ch: 抱歉，刚才是我在自言自语，让你产生了误会。目前列车上十分安全，没有…不对，只有我一个外来人员。
t_en: Oh, sorry, I was thinking out loud. Don't worry, the Express is perfectly safe — there are no... Come to think of it, I'm an unregistered visitor.

t_ch: 尽管我相信星穹列车以及列车上其他乘客的力量，但是基础安防还是不能疏忽的。
t_en: I'm sure you guys and the Express itself are more than capable of maintaining security... but it's important not to overlook basic prevention methods.

t_ch: 购入什么新设备？
t_en: Security equipment...?

t_ch: 因为之前「军团」的入侵，小姐她最近热衷于定制防卫产品，似乎还成为了一些品牌的高级会员。
t_en: Ah, that's right. Since the Legion's invasion, Lady Asta's been procuring defense products customized to the station's needs. I think she's already got platinum membership cards with some of the suppliers...

t_ch: 虽然面对突发状况，个人能力是最关键的因素。但如果有合适的防卫设备在旁，总归会更安全些。
t_en: When the unthinkable happens, it usually comes down to the combat effectiveness of the defenders to gain the upper hand. But it's always helpful to have the right equipment by your side.

t_ch: 除开人员识别系统，我还针对第三方电波监测以及紧急防卫反应装置做了一些记录。
t_en: In addition to visitor recognition systems, I've made a few notes on third-party signal monitoring and emergency response devices.

t_ch: 希望这些记录可以给列车长提供一些新的思路，以备不时之需。
t_en: Of course, it'd be great if you never had to use any of it, but I hope my analysis is of help to the conductor!

t_ch: 知道了，阿兰大哥！
t_en: Roger that, brother Arlan!

t_ch: 那…那只是那帮家伙的玩笑话！你不要当真，也不要跟着他们学！
t_en: I... Th—Those guys aren't serious when they refer to me like that, y'know. Anyway, please don't start using it yourself too...

t_ch: 别在列车上聊工作…
t_en: We have a rule on the Express — no work talk...

t_ch: 抱歉…我只是希望能帮到你。
t_en: Sorry... I was just trying to help.

t_ch: 很感谢{NICKNAME}邀请我来列车上参观。虽然之前也在月台看见过列车几次，但是进入到车厢内…还是第一次。
t_en: Anyway, thanks for inviting me to see the train, {NICKNAME}. I've seen the Express when it was docked at the platform, but this is my first time on board.

t_ch: 我不像小姐那么博学多识……我能做的，就只有在防卫这件事上发挥一些作用了吧？
t_en: I'm not as knowledgeable as Lady Asta... The only way I can contribute is to help the station improve its defenses, don't you think?

t_ch: 出来玩还是放松些吧！
t_en: Thanks, but you're supposed to be off work!

t_ch: 原…原来是这样吗？看来的确是我精神太紧张了。
t_en: H—Huh? *sigh* You're right, I need to learn to unwind.

t_ch: 之前我一直都埋头于空间站的防卫工作，差点忘记劳逸结合才是最有效率的方法了。
t_en: I've been so focused on the station's defenses recently — it's easy to forget that work-life balance is the best way to stay on top.

t_ch: 偶尔放松放松，感觉也很不错…谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thanks for your advice, {NICKNAME}. It's nice to have a break every now and then.

t_ch: 这里可以添加些设备……
t_en: Some devices can be deployed here...

t_ch: 参观的话，应该从哪里开始呢…好不容易放松紧绷的神经，竟然还有点不习惯了。
t_en: Sightseeing on the Express... Where should I begin? It's weird that when I finally have the chance to take a break, I don't know how.

t_ch: 放松是没有标准的！
t_en: Start anywhere you like!

t_ch: 你说得没错。老是考虑用标准衡量问题，是没有办法以休闲的态度参观列车的。
t_en: You're right. I won't enjoy myself if I turn it into another safety inspection.

t_ch: 偶尔放松放松，感觉也很不错…谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thanks for your advice, {NICKNAME}. It's nice to have a break every now and then.

t_ch: 想不到那样小的身躯里，竟然潜藏着如此巨大的能量！
t_en: I didn't know so much power could fit into such a small body!

t_ch: 你是在说我吗？
t_en: ...You talkin' to me?

t_ch: 嘿嘿…夸得我都有点害羞了。
t_en: Ah, stop it, you're making me blush.

t_ch: 啊…抱歉{NICKNAME}，我是在说这辆列车的列车长——帕姆大人。但{NICKNAME}你也是很厉害的。
t_en: Uh... Sorry, {NICKNAME}, I was talking about Pom-Pom, the conductor. But hey, you're super good at what you do, too!

t_ch: 这辆列车行驶于宇宙之中，潜在的危机多不胜数。而帕姆大人不仅确保了列车的安全，甚至还将车厢内的保洁、绿植的养护工作都揽到自己的职责中去。
t_en: The Express must have encountered huge dangers on its journey through the universe. Not only does Pom-Pom make sure that no harm comes to the crew, they even manage to find time to clean the carriages and take care of the plants.

t_ch: 宏观和微观都能把控得如此细致入微…实在是太厉害了！
t_en: Strategically minded with an eye for detail... So impressive.

t_ch: 实在是太了不起了……
t_en: That's really amazing...

t_ch: 这辆列车行驶于宇宙之中，潜在的危机多不胜数。而帕姆大人不仅确保了列车的安全，甚至还将车厢内的保洁、绿植的养护工作都揽到自己的职责中去。
t_en: The Express must have encountered huge dangers on its journey through the universe. Not only does Pom-Pom make sure that no harm comes to the crew, they even manage to find time to clean the carriages and take care of the plants.

t_ch: 宏观和微观都能把控得如此细致入微…实在是太厉害了！
t_en: Strategically minded with an eye for detail... So impressive.

t_ch: 列车里弥漫着的浓郁咖啡香…闻起来真不错。
t_en: *sniff* Is that coffee...? Smells good.

t_ch: 这就是科学的芬芳。
t_en: That's the smell of science happening.

t_ch: 是从姬子的房间传来的。
t_en: That's Himeko's room.

t_ch: 之前列车长告诉我说，这是姬子女士自研咖啡的特殊香味。可惜我喝了咖啡就会失眠，不然我真想向姬子女士求一下配方。
t_en: I remember the conductor telling me that Himeko's coffee had a special aroma. If caffeine didn't mess with my sleeping pattern so much, I'd ask her for the recipe.

t_ch: 空间站很多科员喜欢喝咖啡，他们说这样有利于集中精神做实验。
t_en: A lot of the researchers on the station live off the stuff. They said it helps them focus.

t_ch: 但是他们平时喝的咖啡都没有这种特殊香味。或许这就是姬子女士自研秘方的魅力所在吧？
t_en: Still, none of the coffee they drink has the same aroma as Himeko's brew. She must have a few secret ingredients up her sleeve.

t_ch: 不知道艾丝妲小姐会不会喜欢这样的气味呢？她偶尔也会喝咖啡，不过一定要加糖加奶，她不爱喝苦涩的东西……
t_en: I wonder if Lady Asta would like it? She drinks a cup or two occasionally, but she has it with milk and sugar. She can't stand bitter flavors...

t_ch: 抱歉，好像说到别的地方去了。{NICKNAME}，请别在意。
t_en: Oh, uh, what were we talking about again? Coffee! Sorry, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 浓郁的咖啡香……
t_en: The thick aroma of coffee...

t_ch: 空间站很多科员热衷于喝咖啡，他们说这样有利于集中精神做实验。
t_en: A lot of the researchers on the station live off coffee. They said it helps them focus.

t_ch: 但是他们平时喝的咖啡都没有这种特殊香味。或许这就是姬子女士自研秘方的魅力所在吧？
t_en: Still, none of the coffee they drink has the same aroma as Himeko's brew. She must have a few secret ingredients up her sleeve.

t_ch: 不知道艾丝妲小姐会不会喜欢这样的气味呢？她偶尔也会喝咖啡，不过一定要加糖加奶，她不爱喝苦涩的东西……
t_en: I wonder if Lady Asta would like it? She drinks a cup or two occasionally, but she has it with milk and sugar. She can't stand bitter flavors...

t_ch: 抱歉，好像说到别的地方去了。{NICKNAME}，请别在意。
t_en: Oh, uh, what were we talking about again? Coffee! Sorry, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: …改变一下佩佩的饮食配方，也许就不会掉毛了…
t_en: ...Maybe if I change Peppy's diet, he won't shed so much...

t_ch: 佩佩也来了？
t_en: Is Peppy here, too?

t_ch: 没有没有，毕竟我是受到邀请才有幸来到列车，贸然带一只狗狗过来，或许会给你们添麻烦。
t_en: No, no, I wouldn't bring Peppy without letting you guys know first. You have a lot on your plate — I wouldn't want you to worry about having to take care of him.

t_ch: 在准备什么好吃的？
t_en: Peppy's diet?

t_ch: 之前和列车长简单聊了几句之后，对于佩佩每日饮食的配方有了一些改进的想法。应该谈不上好吃，但一定会更有营养的。
t_en: Yeah, the conductor gave me some ideas on how to improve it. It'll probably taste worse, but it'll be more nutritious.

t_ch: 我看列车上也没有养宠物，但…也有一些掉毛…呃…我猜测可能是列车长有类似的情况，所以询问了它。
t_en: I knew there were no pets on the Express, but I saw signs of shedding. That's when I realized that, uh... maybe the conductor was having the same problem. That's why I wanted to get Pom-Pom's advice.

t_ch: 在得知佩佩的事情之后，列车长很热情地分享了一些独家烹饪配方，说是可以有效缓解这类问题。
t_en: After I told Pom-Pom about Peppy's shedding, they shared some cooking tips that should help solve the problem.

t_ch: 等回去之后我就给佩佩试试，希望它不要挑食。
t_en: I'll start him on the new diet when I get back... Hope he isn't too picky.

t_ch: 从食物下手或许有帮助……
t_en: It might help to start with food...

t_ch: 在得知佩佩的事情之后，列车长很热情地分享了一些独家烹饪配方，说是可以有效缓解这类问题。
t_en: After I told Pom-Pom about Peppy's shedding, they shared some cooking tips that should help solve the problem.

t_ch: 等回去之后我就给佩佩试试，希望它不要挑食。
t_en: I'll start Peppy on the new diet when I get back... Hope he isn't too picky.

t_ch: 如果是这样的坐垫，说不定可以减少身体的负担。
t_en: Now this type of cushion looks like it'd help with fatigue. Y'know, from sitting too long?

t_ch: 坐着不如躺着。
t_en: My advice — stop sitting and start lying down.

t_ch: 躺下的话更适合休息，如果不得不办公，还是只能考虑坐着。
t_en: That's fine if you're not on the clock, but you can't just lie down at work!

t_ch: 我也喜欢坐这儿。
t_en: It's pretty great for sitting.

t_ch: 是很柔软的材质，让人感觉身心都得到了放松。
t_en: The material is super soft — whoever made this had relaxation in mind.

t_ch: 科员们经常需要坐在椅子上办公，有的时候也会抱怨肩颈酸痛。
t_en: Sadly a lot of our researchers get shoulder and neck pain — they're sat at their desks most of the day.

t_ch: 虽然小姐已经为大家购买了人体工学的座椅，但依然觉得差了点什么。如果能使用这个材质的坐垫，应该可以让小姐和科员们都感觉轻松一些。
t_en: Lady Asta bought ergonomic chairs for everybody, but I feel like there's still room for improvement. Cushions like this one might actually be the missing piece.

t_ch: 没想到在参观列车的时候还能帮上大家的忙……
t_en: Who'd have thought I'd get ideas for office furniture while sightseeing on the Express...

t_ch: 这个材质感觉不错……
t_en: This material feels good...

t_ch: 科员们经常需要坐在椅子上办公，有的时候也会抱怨肩颈酸痛。
t_en: Sadly a lot of our researchers get shoulder and neck pain — they're sat at their desks most of the day.

t_ch: 虽然小姐已经为大家购买了人体工学的座椅，但依然觉得差了点什么。如果能使用这个材质的坐垫，应该可以让小姐和科员们都感觉轻松一些。
t_en: Lady Asta bought ergonomic chairs for everybody, but I feel like there's still room for improvement. Cushions like this one might actually be the missing piece.

t_ch: 没想到在参观列车的时候还能帮上大家的忙……
t_en: Who'd have thought I'd get ideas for office furniture while sightseeing on the Express...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，谢谢你邀请我来这里参观列车，放松神经。
t_en: Thanks for inviting me to the Express, {NICKNAME} — it's been nice to have a break.

t_ch: 之前我一直都在思考：防卫科还能做些什么，才能防止「军团」入侵那样的恶性事件再次发生……
t_en: When I got the invitation I was completely caught up in Security Department affairs — how to prevent another Legion invasion, that kind of thing...

t_ch: 不知不觉，精神就处于高度紧张状态，思维也僵化了。但是来到这里之后，看到车窗外的宇宙浩瀚无垠，整个人也感觉舒缓了很多……
t_en: It took me a while to realize I was letting the whole thing stress me out — I couldn't think straight. When I set foot on the Express and saw how vast the universe is, I started to feel calm and relaxed.

t_ch: 虽然不贵重，但这是我能想到最能帮上你的东西，请你务必收下。
t_en: Here, take this. It's nothing expensive, but I'm sure it'll be of help.

t_ch: 谢谢你邀请我来。
t_en: Thank you for inviting me here.

t_ch: 思维的放松果然也很重要…换一换环境竟然可以有如此大的效果。
t_en: It's important to slow down and unwind... A change of scenery is sometimes the best medicine.

t_ch: 之前都太过沉浸于防卫科的工作，忘记休息的重要性了。
t_en: I was too caught up in Security Department affairs — I forgot how important it is to get some good rest.

t_ch: 谢谢你让我想起这一点，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thanks for reminding me, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我得感谢丹恒的邀请，这趟列车之旅当真不虚此行。商人的星际跃迁大多枯燥乏味，很少有这种旅行的感觉。
t_en: I must thank Dan Heng for his invitation — this journey upon the Astral Express has been well worth my while. Interstellar warp jumps with merchants are often dull, and rarely ever feel like an actual journey.

t_ch: 列车…{RUBY_B#阿基维利}游云天君{RUBY_E#}真是位有情调的星神。
t_en: The Astral Express... The {RUBY_B#Akivili}Cloudstrider{RUBY_E#} really was an Aeon of taste.

t_ch: 不是，你哪位啊？
t_en: ...Who are you?

t_ch: 啊…抱歉，还未自我介绍。我是星际行商，在仙舟的注册名是「罗刹」。
t_en: Oh... Forgive me, I forgot to introduce myself. I'm an interstellar merchant — my Xianzhou registered name is "Luocha."

t_ch: 前些日子在罗浮认识了你们列车上的丹恒兄弟，他邀请我得空时来列车上玩。
t_en: I met an acquaintance of yours several days ago named Dan Heng. He invited me to board the Express should I have the time, and so here I am.

t_ch: 真不错啊，这复古的列车设计，这舒适的车厢，唔，如果我们的飞船能这么改造就好了……
t_en: Astounding! The retro design of this locomotive, the comfort of its cabins! If only our own spacecraft could be modified in such a way...

t_ch: 那就改造呗！
t_en: So? Modify it!

t_ch: 哈哈，可不行啊。行商的星际飞船更注重载重、安保、消毒等等事宜……
t_en: Hah, now that would be something... but on a merchant ship, load, security, and sterilization take precedence over creature comforts...

t_ch: 舒适性反倒在其次，主要还是做生意么。我们一般都用公司的量产运输舰。
t_en: What I mean to say is, comfort comes second to business. We generally use the Company's mass-produced transport ships.

t_ch: 说实话没你想得那么好…
t_en: To be honest, it's not as good as you think.

t_ch: 是吗？我听丹恒的描述，还以为你们都很喜欢呢……
t_en: Really? After listening to Dan Heng, I assumed you all liked it...

t_ch: 想象一下，在一列长夜孤灯似的列车里眺望窗外的文明。纷争、痛苦、绝望、悲鸣，仿佛都被隔绝在了另一个世界……
t_en: Such a train can be likened to a solitary candle in the darkness of night... One may peer upon civilizations where strife, hardship, desperation, and sorrow exist seemingly in a separate plane of existence...

t_ch: 想象一下，在一列长夜孤灯似的列车里眺望窗外的文明。纷争、痛苦、绝望、悲鸣，仿佛都被隔绝在了另一个世界……
t_en: Such a train can be likened to a solitary candle in the darkness of night... One may peer upon civilizations where strife, hardship, desperation, and sorrow exist seemingly in a separate plane of existence...

t_ch: 真是绝景啊……
t_en: It truly is a stunning view...

t_ch: 列车里的人看见我都很惊讶呢。
t_en: People on the Express are shocked at the sight of me.

t_ch: 为啥？
t_en: Why's that?

t_ch: 惊讶什么？
t_en: What's so shocking?

t_ch: 我就不惊讶。
t_en: I'm not shocked.

t_ch: 听说在丹恒兄弟上车前，列车里有个跟我长得很像的乘客。
t_en: I heard Dan Heng mention that before he boarded the Express, there was a passenger who looked remarkably like me.

t_ch: 人好像不怎么样，给他们留下了心理阴影。
t_en: He was seemingly up to no good and left a lasting psychological scar.

t_ch: 那位戴眼镜的瓦尔特先生，看见我后转身走了，过了一会儿拿设备传了本书给我，叫《论环境的意义——没有人生而为恶》。
t_en: The gentleman in spectacles — Mr. Welt Yang — he walked away as soon as he laid eyes on me. A while later he used a device to transfer a book to me called "On The Matter of Environment - No One Is Born Evil."

t_ch: …那个和我长得很像的人一定很有趣吧。
t_en: ...I'll wager that fellow was an intriguing character.

t_ch: …那个和我长得很像的人一定很有趣吧。
t_en: ...I'll wager that fellow was an intriguing character.
*sigh*

t_ch: 你看我干啥！
t_en: Why are you looking at me?

t_ch: 你叹什么气！
t_en: What's with the sighing?

t_ch: 你有话直说！
t_en: If you have something to say, say it!

t_ch: 啊，没有冒犯的意思。在下略懂医术，观你的行气节奏，感觉脉象有异。
t_en: Goodness! I don't mean to offend... I happen to have some medical knowledge, and while observing the flow of your qi, I noticed something unusual about your pulse.

t_ch: 什么意思！
t_en: What's that supposed to mean!?

t_ch: 你的身体不大健康。
t_en: You're not in the best health.

t_ch: 你靠谱吗？
t_en: Can I trust your judgment?

t_ch: 也算…靠谱吧。
t_en: ...I would say so.

t_ch: 我还有几天…
t_en: How long have I got left?

t_ch: 没那么严重……
t_en: It's not that serious...

t_ch: 长期在宇宙中旅行的人，人体生物周期多少有些紊乱，属于太空病的一种。
t_en: Those who travel the vastness of the universe for extended periods will often find their bodily cycles to be in some sort of disarray. It's a form of space sickness.

t_ch: 我开个安神方子，睡前服用，最好能按一定的规律安排稳定的作息。
t_en: Let me prescribe you something to soothe your body and mind — take it before bed. It's best that you establish a regular work and sleep schedule.

t_ch: 神医救我！
t_en: Thank you, o miracle doctor!

t_ch: 呃，谈不上、谈不上。
t_en: Ahem, you are truly too kind...

t_ch: …你的方子靠谱吗？
t_en: Can I trust your medicine?

t_ch: 这个…你姑且信其有吧。
t_en: Well, again, I would say so...

t_ch: 我几乎都不睡觉…
t_en: I hardly ever sleep...

t_ch: 那我也爱莫能助了。
t_en: Well, in that case there is little I can do.

t_ch: 保护好身体。
t_en: Take care of yourself.

t_ch: 保护好身体。
t_en: Take care of yourself.

t_ch: 有个问题不知当问不当……
t_en: I have a question for you that I am hesitant to ask...

t_ch: 但说无妨。
t_en: There's no harm in asking.

t_ch: 问呗。
t_en: Ask away.

t_ch: 在你附近时，我能感受到一种力量…唔，难道你手上有颗「星核」吗？
t_en: Whenever I am in your vicinity, I sense a sort of... power. Could it be you have a Stellaron in your possession?

t_ch: 你是怎么知道的？
t_en: How did you know?

t_ch: 在下去过被万界之癌污染的世界，靠近它的那种感觉，和靠近你时很像。
t_en: It is a feeling I have encountered when approaching worlds that have been polluted by the Cancer of All Worlds...

t_ch: 丹恒这都告诉你？
t_en: Dan Heng told you this too?

t_ch: 不不，你误会丹恒兄弟了，这是我自己的猜测。
t_en: You are mistaken. Dan Heng told me nothing of the sort — this was my own conjecture.

t_ch: 我没有，你别瞎说。
t_en: What nonsense!

t_ch: 啊，那大概是在下弄错了吧。
t_en: Ah? Then I am most likely mistaken.

t_ch: 不过，感觉虽然很像，却意外地沉静，没有万界之癌那种狂暴和贪婪……
t_en: The sense I speak of is familiar to me, yet unexpectedly characterized by a calmness. It lacks the fervor and greed that the Cancer of All Worlds possesses.

t_ch: （他的直觉好准啊……）
t_en: (He has a strong intuition...)

t_ch: 感觉虽然很像……
t_en: The sense is familiar to me, and yet...

t_ch: 是黑塔的奇物收藏地啊……
t_en: Herta's Curio collection...

t_ch: 这里就是空间站「黑塔」吗，不知访客能否入内？
t_en: So, this is the Herta Space Station? I wonder, are visitors permitted onboard?

t_ch: 据说不行，有门禁。
t_en: I heard there were restrictions.

t_ch: 重建中，闲人止步。
t_en: It's under reconstruction, no entry for unauthorized personnel.

t_ch: 这样啊，太可惜了。
t_en: Is that so? What a shame.

t_ch: 黑塔女士最近很有名呢，公司的节目和广播里轰炸般地反复提及她的名字。
t_en: Madam Herta's reputation precedes her of late — her name is constantly mentioned in the company's broadcasts and programs.

t_ch: 据说她是以利亚萨拉斯之后，第一个愿意和世人分享才华的俱乐部天才。
t_en: Prior to her, they say that the last member of the Genius Society who was willing to showcase their talents was Elias Salas.

t_ch: 以利亚萨拉斯让全银河的科技水平获得了一次飞跃，这次黑塔女士的出现，或许也会是一次科技革命……
t_en: Elias Salas's contributions ushered forth a great galaxy-wide technological leap. Now that Madam Herta is on the scene, perhaps another technological revolution looms...

t_ch: 以利亚萨拉斯是谁啊？
t_en: Who is Elias Salas?

t_ch: 很久以前的一位天才，听说超距遥感和心灵通感信标都是他的发明，只是署名让给了博识学会。
t_en: A genius from long ago. It is said that he invented ultraremote sensing and the telepathy beacon, only he signed them over to the Intelligentsia Guild.

t_ch: 在众多俱乐部天才里，以利亚萨拉斯算是个异类。他不愿意人为增寿，活了一百零三岁就去世了。据说他至少有一百种延长生命的办法，但他就是不用。
t_en: Out of the many members of the Genius Society, you could say Elias Salas was... different. He didn't want to extend his lifespan, and so passed away at the age of <unbreak>103</unbreak>. It's estimated he had at least a hundred different methods for prolonging life, yet he chose not to use them.

t_ch: 黑塔有那么厉害吗？
t_en: Is Herta really that awesome?

t_ch: 她是天才俱乐部的人。那个俱乐部只有被博识尊认可的存在才能加入，是精英中的精英，亿亿无一的天才。
t_en: She's a member of the Genius Society. Only those approved by Nous may join — the smartest of the smart... her genius is one in a trillion.

t_ch: 这群人至少有半数把名字留在了宇宙史上：利尔他、「帝皇」鲁珀特、以利亚萨拉斯、原始博士、螺丝咕姆……
t_en: At least half the members of the Society have etched their names on the universe's wall of history — there's Aiden, Emperor Rubert, Elias Salas, Dr. Primitive, Screwllum...

t_ch: 你知道得不少嘛。
t_en: You seem to know quite a lot, don't you?

t_ch: 哈哈，商人嘛，信息也是商品的一种。
t_en: Haha, what can I say, I'm a merchant, and information is also a commodity.

t_ch: 真希望有机会能去空间站看看啊。
t_en: I really hope I get the chance to look around the space station.

t_ch: 真希望有机会能去空间站看看啊。
t_en: I really hope I get the chance to look around the space station.

t_ch: 常来列车叨扰各位，这里有些薄礼，聊表谢意。
t_en: I hope my presence here has not proved a nuisance to you all. Here, a few meager gifts as tokens of my appreciation.

t_ch: 那我就不跟你客气了。
t_en: Well, then, I'll gladly take whatever you've got.

t_ch: 没事，大家都自己人。
t_en: The least I could do.

t_ch: 还是感谢阿基维利吧。
t_en: Better to give thanks to Akivili.

t_ch: 收下吧。它们虽不算昂贵，但也有点用处。
t_en: Please, take them. They're not exactly valuable, but they have their uses.

t_ch: 这怎么好意思…
t_en: I really shouldn't...

t_ch: 客气了。
t_en: You're welcome.

t_ch: 谢谢，收下了。
t_en: Thank you, I'll take them then.

t_ch: 我感谢你们才是。
t_en: I should be thanking you.

t_ch: 下次来点昂贵的。
t_en: Then, next time, bring me more valuable stuff!

t_ch: 呵呵，好。
t_en: Hehe, well noted...

t_ch: 乘坐星穹列车徜徉宇宙…真令人羡慕啊。
t_en: Wandering the universe on the Astral Express... *sigh* One can only be envious!

t_ch: 乘坐星穹列车徜徉宇宙…真令人羡慕啊。
t_en: Wandering the universe on the Astral Express... *sigh* One can only be envious!

t_ch: 一些薄礼，聊表谢意……
t_en: A few meager gifts as tokens of my appreciation...

t_ch: 哈喽。
t_en: Hello.

t_ch: 你眨了眨眼，再次确认自己看见了什么——的确是她，银狼，一个按理不该出现在这儿的人。
t_en: You blinked your eyes and made sure that the person you just saw was Silver Wolf — she wasn't supposed to be here.

t_ch: 你怎么会在这里？！
t_en: What are you doing on the Express!?

t_ch: 怎么了？别人都能上这车，我也能来。
t_en: What do you mean what am I doing? Everyone else is here, can't I be too?

t_ch: （装作没看见）
t_en: (Pretend you didn't notice her)

t_ch: 怎么了？一副看见电子蜗牛的表情。
t_en: What's wrong with you? You look like you just got shocked by an electric snail.

t_ch: 三月七，快逃！
t_en: March 7th, quick, make a run for it!

t_ch: 还挺有同伴意识，我喜欢。
t_en: A strong sense of awareness for your companions — I like that.

t_ch: 丹恒，快逃！
t_en: Dan Heng, watch out! Make a run for it!

t_ch: 还挺有同伴意识，我喜欢。
t_en: A strong sense of awareness for your companions — I like that.

t_ch: 安心吧，只是来串个门。我正在进攻星际互联网络的黑域，碰巧在网络迷宫看见了星穹列车的信号，就来瞧一眼。
t_en: Relax, I'm just dropping by for a visit. I was hacking through the dark regions of the Interastral Network a while ago and happened to see a signal from the Astral Express, so I thought I'd stop by to check it out.

t_ch: 站在这儿的只是个全息投影，啥也干不了，跟车厢顶上的虚空鲸没什么区别。你看——
t_en: What you see in front of you is a hologram, just as harmless as the whale holograms you see floating around the Express. Here, see for yourself...

t_ch: 她伸手戳了戳你的腹部，手指径直消失在了衣服表面。
t_en: She poked your stomach. The part of her finger that touched your clothes disappeared.

t_ch: 这下相信了吧？要是还不放心，你可以断开网络连接，那样就看不到我了——除非你把它再连上。
t_en: Seeing is believing, right? If you're still not convinced, you can cut off the network connection on the Express and I'll disappear into thin air — until you turn it back on, of course.

t_ch: 站在这儿的只是个全息投影，啥也干不了，你不会还要我再证明一次？
t_en: What you see in front of you is just a hologram, harmless as a puff of air. You don't need another demonstration, do you?

t_ch: 哈喽。
t_en: Hello.

t_ch: 要不要玩点什么？桌面游戏我也很擅长。
t_en: Want to play a game? I'm pretty good at board games, you know.

t_ch: 你不是立体投影吗？
t_en: Aren't you using a hologram, though?

t_ch: 哦对，我摸不到棋子…什么年代了，还在用实体棋盘，无名客也太怀旧了。
t_en: Oh yeah, holograms can't move board game pieces... The Nameless must really be a nostalgic group to be using physical board games in this modern age.

t_ch: 你没别的事可做吗？
t_en: Don't you have anything else to do?

t_ch: 有啊，一边和你聊天，一边分三个屏幕玩不同的游戏，顺带打探下游牧矿工的消息，赚点零花钱。
t_en: Oh, I have plenty to do. As I chat here with you on the Express, I'm also playing multiple games at once on three different screens, probing around for info about nomadic miners, and running some side-hustles for extra cash.

t_ch: 你们这么大个车厢，连台街机都没有，真好奇你们平时都是怎么打发时间的，读书？拍照？喝咖啡？东奔西走助人为乐？
t_en: You all have so much space here on the Express, and not so much as a single arcade machine to show for it. How do you pass the time? Reading? Photography? Sipping coffee? Running around everywhere helping people as a hobby?

t_ch: 要不你叫几个朋友，我教你们玩「星际十三步」——聚会游戏我也很擅长。
t_en: Why don't you call over some friends, and I'll teach you how to play Thirteen Star-Steps - I'm good at party games too, you know.

t_ch: 你们这么大个车厢，连台街机都没有，真好奇你们平时都是怎么打发时间的，读书？拍照？喝咖啡？东奔西走助人为乐？
t_en: You all have so much space here on the Express, and not so much as a single arcade machine to show for it. How do you pass the time? Reading? Photography? Sipping coffee? Running around everywhere helping people as a hobby?

t_ch: 要不你叫几个朋友，我教你们玩「星际十三步」——聚会游戏我也很擅长。
t_en: Why don't you call over some friends, and I'll teach you how to play Thirteen Star-Steps - I'm good at party games too, you know.

t_ch: 要不要玩点什么？
t_en: Want to play a game?

t_ch: 有点意思，这车里的设施还挺齐全：前厅、休息室、还有每个人的包间，难怪无名客能四海为家。
t_en: Interesting. The facilities on the Express are quite extensive: With a reception room, a lounge, and a private coach for each passenger, it's no wonder the Nameless are able to call the four corners of the galaxy their home.

t_ch: 人生就是场没有终点的旅行。
t_en: Life is but a journey without a destination.

t_ch: 入戏倒挺快——你上车没多久吧。
t_en: You're a new passenger yourself, aren't you?

t_ch: 要不要留下来？
t_en: Sounds like you wanna join the Express!

t_ch: 不行，星核猎手那边的「游戏」还没通关呢，总得有个先来后到。
t_en: I'm afraid that won't be feasible — I'm still busy playing the Stellaron Hunters' game at the moment. First come first serve, after all.

t_ch: 星核猎手不是这样吗？
t_en: So a Stellaron Hunter's life is not like this?

t_ch: 这我不能告诉你，会被卡芙卡念叨的。
t_en: I cannot comment on such matters, or I will be reprimanded by Kafka.

t_ch: 离开朋克洛德前，我也曾梦想过当个无名客——乘着列车离开星球，等待着我的将是新手村外广阔无垠的冒险。
t_en: Before I left Punklorde, I also dreamed of joining the Nameless and hopping on the Express to travel between worlds — beyond the village of newbies back home, a vast and endless adventure awaited me.

t_ch: 当然，我也可以选择加入公司，或者巡海游侠，甚至利用一下泯灭帮，只要能让我奔向银河，走哪条路都可以。
t_en: Of course, I could have also joined the IPC, the Galaxy Rangers, or even the Annihilation Gang. Whatever road takes me across the galaxy is the one I'm willing to travel on.

t_ch: 但最后是星核猎手先找到了我——或者说，是我找到了他们。用艾利欧的话讲，这就是「命运」。
t_en: But in the end, it was the Stellaron Hunters who found me first — or rather, it was I who found them. In Elio's words, this is called destiny.

t_ch: 离开朋克洛德前，我也曾梦想过当个无名客——乘着列车离开星球，等待着我的将是新手村外广阔无垠的冒险。
t_en: Before I left Punklorde, I also dreamed of joining the Nameless and hopping on the Express to travel between worlds — beyond the village of newbies back home, a vast and endless adventure awaited me.

t_ch: 当然，我也可以选择加入公司，或者巡海游侠，甚至利用一下泯灭帮，只要能让我奔向银河，走哪条路都可以。
t_en: Of course, I could have also joined the IPC, the Galaxy Rangers, or even the Annihilation Gang. Whatever road takes me across the galaxy is the one I'm willing to travel on.

t_ch: 但最后是星核猎手先找到了我——或者说，是我找到了他们。用艾利欧的话讲，这就是「命运」。
t_en: But in the end, it was the Stellaron Hunters who found me first — or rather, it was I who found them. In Elio's words, this is called destiny.

t_ch: 有点意思……
t_en: Interesting...

t_ch: 哦，{NICKNAME}，表情很严肃啊。你这是看见了什么？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, that's a serious expression you've got there. Did something catch your attention?

t_ch: 你。
t_en: You did.

t_ch: 入侵者。
t_en: Intruder.

t_ch: 你在说谁？哦，是我啊……
t_en: Who are you calling an intruder? Oh, me...

t_ch: 我只是来串门的，没有恶意。
t_en: I mean no harm — I'm just visiting.

t_ch: 之前那位戴眼镜的大叔也在用很微妙的眼神看我，我们之间是不是哪里有误会？
t_en: The older gentleman with glasses was also giving me some very subtle looks. Has there been a misunderstanding of some kind?

t_ch: 还是说，你们在去别人家做客前，难道会和对方先讲一声？
t_en: Do you always tell people ahead of time before you pay them a visit?

t_ch: 难道不是吗？
t_en: That's called common courtesy.

t_ch: 无名客向来礼貌待人！
t_en: The Nameless have always been hospitable hosts.

t_ch: 我明白了，这是习惯差异。朋克洛德没这传统，星核猎手一般也不这么做事。
t_en: I see, it's just a cultural difference. Punklorde doesn't have this custom, and the Stellaron Hunters don't generally observe this kind of tradition either.

t_ch: 电影里不是经常有那种主人回家开灯，却发现早有人坐在沙发椅上等他的桥段吗？卡芙卡和刃都喜欢这么干，萨姆甚至不会让他开灯。
t_en: Isn't it often the case in movies that a guy comes home and turns on the lights, only to find someone sitting on the sofa waiting for him? Kafka and Blade both like to do that, and Sam won't even give the guy a chance to turn on the lights.

t_ch: 但我就不一样了，我会在对方进门前就主动打招呼。你看，我对你还是不错的。
t_en: But that's not my style. I will take the initiative to say hello before the other person enters the room. You see, I'm treating you quite well.

t_ch: 放心，来拜访的时候，我都会主动和你打招呼的。
t_en: No need to worry — I will always take the initiative to greet you when I come to visit.

t_ch: 发生了什么？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 呜，糟了，好难受…早知道应该关闭重力模拟的……
t_en: Ooh, drats, what a drag... should have known to turn off the gravity simulator...

t_ch: 习惯了网络漫游，反而不适应正常的交通工具了……
t_en: I've grown so accustomed to network roaming that normal transportation feels weird now.

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: （装作没看见）
t_en: (Pretend you didn't notice her)
Who's there!?

t_ch: 啊，是你啊…你看到了？
t_en: Ah, it's you... you saw that?

t_ch: 你这是…晕车了？
t_en: Are you... suffering from motion sickness?

t_ch: 你也有弱点啊。
t_en: So you do have weaknesses, huh?

t_ch: 求你别吐在车上…
t_en: Try not to vomit on the Express. Please!

t_ch: 我闲着无聊，逗你玩的。晕车？我？怎么可能。
t_en: No, no, I'm just messing with you because I'm bored. Motion sickness? Me? Don't be absurd. Don't forget—

t_ch: 别忘了，站在你面前的只是一个全息投影，真正的我现在正躺在舒服的冰水浴缸里，压根就不在星穹列车上——
t_en: —standing in front of you is just a hologram. The real me is now lying in a cozy ice bath, far, far away from the Astral Express.

t_ch: 喂——喂喂——
t_en: Hello? Hello...?

t_ch: 列车即将开启跃迁，请各位乘客做好准备——
t_en: The Express is about to warp jump, all passengers please get ready!

t_ch: 人呢？！
t_en: Where did she go?

t_ch: 逃跑了？！
t_en: She made a run for it?

t_ch: 你沉思片刻，确信自己发现了银狼的弱点，以及——
t_en: You thought for a while. You were sure that you've found Silver Wolf's weakness and —

t_ch: 她刚刚其实就在列车上。
t_en: —She was on the Express for real, not just a hologram.

t_ch: 那个熟悉的全息投影又回来了。
t_en: That familiar hologram is back.

t_ch: 你揉了揉眼睛，刚刚的一切仿佛都是一场幻觉。
t_en: You rub your eyes, and everything that transpired just now seems like an illusion.

t_ch: 呜，糟了……
t_en: Oh no, this is bad...

t_ch: 很反常地，银狼没有向你打招呼，而是竖起食指，聚精会神地在空中点击着什么。
t_en: Silver Wolf didn't greet you as usual. Instead, she raised her finger and clicked on something in the air.

t_ch: 你在做什么？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 嘘——等会儿，我在忙。
t_en: Shh — wait a minute, I'm busy.

t_ch: 片刻过后，你看见她的脸上洋溢出喜悦的笑容。
t_en: After a while, you see a smile on her happy face.

t_ch: 中了！
t_en: Got it!

t_ch: 哦对，你看不见屏幕，和你解释一下，我刚才在转扭蛋，就是游戏里都会有的那种。
t_en: Oh yeah, you can't see the screen. Let me explain: I was just pulling for a gacha capsule — you know, the kind you can get in games.

t_ch: 至于结果，当然是一次就得了五星——也就是头等奖。
t_en: As for the result, I got a five-star prize in one go — the top prize.

t_ch: 炫耀啥呢？
t_en: Ugh. What a show-off...

t_ch: 你来这就为了这个？
t_en: So that's why you came all the way here? All for a lucky gacha pull spot?

t_ch: 这你就不懂了，玩游戏，技术是一回事，运气是另一回事——想要一直赢，就得二者兼顾。
t_en: You simply don't understand. In games, skill is one thing, luck is another — if you want to win all the time, you need to have both.

t_ch: 前者可以靠练习，后者就得看天赋。直觉告诉我，今天观景车厢的运势流向很不错，确认取消三次后转动扭蛋，就是命中目标的最佳时机。
t_en: The former can be acquired through practice, but the latter depends on talent. The flow of luck in the Parlor Car is very favorable today. The best strategy is to pull for the desired target after confirming and canceling three times in a row.

t_ch: 真的？
t_en: Is that so?

t_ch: 假的吧…
t_en: Sounds dubious...

t_ch: 我去试试！
t_en: Really!? I'mma give it a try right now!

t_ch: 事先说清楚，这只是我的直觉，对你不一定管用，如果没有效果，我不负责。
t_en: Let's be clear about one thing first — this is just my intuition. It may not work for you, and I am not responsible for any sub-optimal results.

t_ch: 总之，今天开心！送你一份小礼物，希望你也能和我一样顺利。
t_en: Anyway, have a happy day! Here's a little gift for you. I hope everything works out as well for you as it has for me.

t_ch: 站在棋盘边，离两侧座椅同样远的点位上，面朝过道对面的观景窗，将视线对准车厢顶部的虚空鲸投影，然后打开界面，确认取消三次后转动扭蛋，就是命中目标的最佳时机。
t_en: Stand at the edge of the board, at a point equidistant from the seats on either side, facing the viewing window opposite the aisle. Align your eyes with the void song whales hologram near the ceiling and open the interface. Then pull for the prize after confirming and canceling three times in a row. This is the best strategy for obtaining the desired prize.

t_ch: 事先说清楚，这只是我的直觉，对你不一定管用，如果没有效果，我不负责。
t_en: Let's be clear about one thing first — this is just my intuition. It may not work for you, and I am not responsible for any sub-optimal results.

t_ch: 坐上「司舵」那张椅子之后，我已经很久没有机会像这样观看天空了……
t_en: Since assuming the role of Helm Master, it's rare that I have the chance to gaze upon the stars like this...

t_ch: 本该是我看厌了的风景，可如今又让我不由得感叹…天空真是高远啊。
t_en: It should be a view I tire of seeing, but it always leaves me in awe... The heavens really are boundless.
